title: Medicago glandulosa
taxonomy: Medicago glandulosa is classified within the family Fabaceae, commonly known as the legume family. This plant is further classified in the subfamily Faboideae and tribe Fabeae. It belongs to the genus Medicago, which comprises a group of annual and perennial legumes. Medicago glandulosa is closely related to other species within the genus, such as Medicago sativa and Medicago lupulina. The classification of Medicago glandulosa reflects its evolutionary relationships with other legumes.

intro: Medicago glandulosa, also known as glandular medick, is a perennial legume plant native to the Mediterranean region. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, such as irritable bowel syndrome, and reduce the risk of cardiovascular disease. The bioactive constituents of Medicago glandulosa include isoflavones, saponins, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is typically used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into supplements and capsules to support overall health and well-being.
